measurements.alignment: hero centered; component/blog content predominantly left-aligned; nav centered within full-width shell
measurements.section_gaps: DOM reports no isolated positive gaps; visual separation comes from 28–112px padding and surface changes

taste.name: Warm paper as the continuous field
taste.trigger: When choosing a site-wide canvas for marketing, catalog, docs, and editorial pages
taste.decision: Chose a warm #FFF7E8 paper field over a neutral #F5F5F5 or dark canvas
taste.reason: The tinted field makes black rules and yellow actions read as printed marks, while the same surface can carry long docs without a theme switch
evidence: #FFF7E8 covers 61.1% of sampled home background area
#6B6355 is the repeated muted text color on all four pages

taste.name: Offset ink replaces soft elevation
taste.trigger: When a card, button, or interactive control needs to separate from the paper
taste.decision: Chose hard black offsets of 2px–4px with 0px blur over diffuse multi-layer shadows
taste.reason: The object keeps a physical cut-out edge at small sizes and remains legible against both white and paper surfaces
evidence: The dominant shadow has no blur or spread

taste.name: Display personality, utility body
taste.trigger: When headings must carry the brand while component labels and docs need fast scanning
taste.decision: Chose Bricolage Grotesque for display levels and Geist for body/UI over one-family typography
taste.reason: The display face makes section changes visible at a glance; Geist keeps 14–18px labels and 16px docs text compact without ornamental letterforms
evidence: Bricolage Grotesque is used for 72/60/48/36/30/24px headings
Geist is used for 18px/28px body and 16px/24px docs
Geist Mono is isolated to code with 14px text

taste.name: Color is a signal, not wallpaper
taste.trigger: When adding emphasis across a large component catalog and long landing page
taste.decision: Chose #FFDC58 for primary emphasis and kept mint/purple/pink to small decorative zones instead of tinting every section
taste.reason: Readers can predict that yellow means action or focus; decorative colors remain occasional markers rather than competing navigation channels
evidence: #FFDC58 is the repeated accent on all four pages
Home accent candidates show yellow 10 times versus black 57 and muted ink 24
Mint #01FFCC, purple #C4A1FF, and pink #FF30CD each stay below 2.2% sampled area
